notes: A single turn aborts MID-INFERENCE with a provider/runtime error and no normal turn-end signal. opencode records the failure on the assistant MESSAGE row (message.data.error), NOT as a step-finish reason=error part — it emits only a bare step-start part on that message — so message.data.error is the sole turn-ending signal. The daemon's watcher.go isErrorMessage reads it as a terminal signal (#493) and settles the turn working→ready; the recipe's transcript export injects it as `_error` so the replay fixture carries it too (driver fix ee477644). Phases assert the canonical ready→working→ready arc where the FINAL ready comes from the error settle, proving the session does NOT stick in `working` after the abort. Trigger: a context-overflow prompt (~6-7k tokens) against the local LM Studio model loaded at n_ctx 4096 → 'n_keep > context length' UnknownError, which lands AFTER a step-start part (the ≥1-part case the daemon can observe; a pre-inference error that emits zero parts is a documented out-of-scope daemon limitation). session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession can't steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; turn1_start (matching working) establishes the session identity turn1_aborted chains off. The `no state_transition to waiting` invariant holds because the errored turn never reaches a tool/permission gate.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch; never enters waiting
PID bound to the ses_… session
User prompt drives the ses_… session ready → working
The turn aborts by error and the session settles back to ready on the same ses_… session — it does not stick in working
